MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER SHORTENING TIME TO HEAR A MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS BARRING TESTIMONY BY PLAINTIFF REGARDING LIABILITY, INJURIES, MEDICAI, TREATMENT, PAIN AND SUFFFRING AND WAGE LOSS AT TRIAL(TRANSACTION ID # 100208039) FILED BY DEFENDANT LAM, JIMMY

DISCOVERY 302, AUG-24-2023. HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JIMMY LAM'S MOTION TO TERMINATE SANCTIONS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO ATTEND HER DEPOSITION. THE MATTER IS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT FINDS THAT EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS ARE WARRANTED GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S UNREASONABLE AND UNJUSTIFIED REFUSAL TO PARTICIPATE IN THE DISCOVERY PROCESS BY APPEARING FOR HER DULY NOTICED DEPOSITION WHICH RESULTS IN PREJUDICE TO THE MOVING PARTY. THE COURT IS INCLINED TO ISSUE AN ORDER BARRING PLAINTIFF DESHAUNA JONES FROM PRESENTING ANY TESTIMONY AS TO LIABILITY, INJURIES, MEDICAL TREATMENT, PAIN AND SUFFERING AND WAGE LOSS AT TRIAL AS WELL AS OTHER ORDERS THAT MAY BE APPROPRIATE. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE PRO TEM JUDGE AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN. CLERK: R. WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Motion To Terminate Sanctions Or In The Alternative Compel Plaintiff To Attend Her Deposition
Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Paula Shaw, Esq., McDowell Shaw & Garcia, (925) 210-1300, for Defendant.

No other appearances.

RE: DEFENDANT JIMMY LAM's Motion To Terminate Sanctions Or In The Alternative Compel Plaintiff To Attend Her Deposition.

The Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem Judge. The pro tem Judge and Defendant's Counsel (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear for a hearing.

The matter is argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling. The Court finds that evidentiary sanctions are warranted given Plaintiff's unreasonable and unjustified refusal to participate in the discovery process by appearing for her duly noticed deposition which results in prejudice to the moving party. The Court is inclined to issue an order barring Plaintiff Deshauna Jones from presenting any testimony as to liability, injuries, medical treatment, pain and suffering and wage loss at trial as well as other orders that may be appropriate.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem Judge at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion To Terminate Sanctions Or In The Alternative Compel Plaintiff To Attend Her Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Continued To Aug-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
David McDowell, Esq. 925-210-1300 McDowell Shaw & Garcia for Defendant Jimmy Lam
No other Appearance.

THE PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. Counsel apprises the Court of attempts to serve the Plaintiff. The Court shares its views, cites appellate case law and informs Counsel, more effort is needed to locate the Plaintiff before seeking ultimate sanctions. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JIMMY LAM's Motion To Terminate Sanctions Or In The Alternative Compel Plaintiff To Attend Her Deposition.

Motion To Terminate Sanctions. Denied without Prejudice.
Motion to Compel Plaintiff To Attend Her Deposition. Continued to 8/24/23 at 9:00am. Counsel to submit a supplemental declaration. Counsel to submit a proposed form of order to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William C. Lynn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
